subject: Re: Local refinement of particles from a 2D lattice in M (MCore)
body:
Hi Juha,

Great question! My understanding is that pose parameters are refined in the
same way as other model parameters, they are optimized directly with the
L-BFGS optimiser.
This means that they are not explicitly constrained, however they should be
refined only very locally and change by small amounts at each iteration.
When I was working on similar data I never had issues with particles
jumping around the lattice in M, although the spacing in my lattice was
relatively large at 12nm.

If you visualize your positions/orientations and notice this is causing
issues please report back!

Cheers,

Alister

On Mon, 16 Dec 2024 at 00:22, Juha Huiskonen <juha.huiskonen@gmail.com>
wrote:

> Hi all,
>
> When particle poses are refined in MCore, how are the search parameters
> (shifts, rotations) limited around the initial pose?
>
> My particles come from a 2D lattice, and I would like to make sure that 1.
> M does not match the neighboring unit and 2. M does not search too far from
> the initial pose (which I know from geometry of the lattice and have
> limited as a prior in RELION). The structure I am currently averaging is a
> patch from this lattice i.e. more than one repeating unit.
>
> Best wishes,
> Juha
>
